args.title: Q1: how should dlktk depend on pudl post-rename?

args.text: Bumping a pin is one 'go get github.com/chazu/pudl@latest' plus a commit — cheap — and deliberate adoption is a feature: you take a pudl change with a reviewable diff, not silently. Same auditability ethos as the rest of dlktk
args.author: Reviewer

args.text: Pinning risks dlktk lagging pudl bugfixes; the factstore surface is young and fixes land often
args.author: Devx

args.text: Tracking main makes every dlktk build depend on pudl's latest unreviewed commit — non-reproducible, and a broken pudl main breaks dlktk CI with no pinned escape hatch. Replay/postmortem especially wants a known-good engine version
args.author: Reviewer

args.text: A gitignored go.work workspace gives the same zero-friction local override WITHOUT committing it, so the inner loop stays fast while committed builds remain reproducible. The replace's only advantage evaporates
args.author: Reviewer

args.text: During tight co-evolution, pin-and-bump means every pudl API change needs a tag plus a dlktk bump: friction that slows the inner loop
args.author: Devx

args.text: A committed replace=>../pudl breaks any build lacking a sibling pudl checkout — CI, fresh clones, and the nous consumer — and silently masks drift between the required version and what actually compiles. Reproducibility lost
args.author: Reviewer

args.text: Drop replace; track pudl main via pseudo-version so changes land immediately
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Drop replace; pin the tagged release (require v0.1.2) and bump deliberately with go get
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Keep the committed replace=>../pudl: zero-friction local co-dev while pudl/dlktk/nous co-evolve
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Now that pudl is renamed and tagged v0.1.2, how should dlktk depend on it: keep the committed replace=>../pudl, drop replace and pin the tag, or drop replace and track main?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: drop the committed replace; pin require v0.1.2 and bump deliberately via go get; use a gitignored go.work for local co-dev (keeps inner-loop speed without breaking reproducible/CI builds)
